items.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids liable for large legal costs after royalties trial

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

items.title: 'Immense breach of human rights': WA strip searches double in three years

children.content: The number of people strip searched by police officers in Western Australia more than doubles in three years, leading some to label the practice a human rights violation.

image.altText: Exterior of Perth Police Centre, Sept 2024

items.title: Dingo control stopped to protect cultural significance

children.content: Farmers voice concern for their flocks after wild dog control is halted on jointly managed conservation land in parts of the Wheatbelt.

image.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.

items.title: Thousands more Perth properties to be subdivided under major shake-up

children.content: Major planning changes will allow more Perth home owners to subdivide their properties in a bid to boost housing supply as the city continues to fall short of its infill target.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a suburban streetscape

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a suburban streetscape

items.title: Parents say latest asbestos find in WA school grounds is one of many

children.content: Parents at a WA primary school say the education department has failed to properly address the ongoing issue of asbestos being found in school playgrounds.

image.altText: catherine mayfield

items.title: No mice found after drone baiting on remote WA island

children.content: A world-first drone-baiting program to protect migratory bird populations shows early signs of success off Western Australia's north coast.

image.altText: A man holding a mousetrap

items.title: As they mourn their daughter, family wants a ‘life for a life’ from accused man

children.content: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by a man from Western Australia places her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

articles.title: Photos show room where police allege Thai teenager was last seen

children.content: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

image.altText: A woman speaks to a man in a room.

articles.title: Man accused of Thai girl's murder grew up and owned firearms in WA, moved eight months ago

children.content: Australian Simon Peter Carman went about "normal life", going out, washing his clothes and talking to neighbours after allegedly murdering a teenage girl and stuffing her body inside a suitcase in Thailand, police say.

image.altText: A man sits in a singlet behind a desk looking at the camera

gallery.altText: a gold urn in a concrete nook with white flowers infront of the urn

thumbnail.altText: a gold urn in a concrete nook with white flowers infront of the urn
Simon has back to camera, wheels a suitcase, grainy photo, tiled hallway, wears black sleeveless top, shorts.

items.title: ACCC blocks Coles supermarket in Kalgoorlie to protect regional city's independents

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog exercises new powers to thwart the opening of a Coles supermarket in the WA outback city, fearing it could knock out smaller competitors in the wider region.

items.title: NDIS fraud 'gaps remain open' as integrity report handed down

children.content: A Labor-led parliamentary inquiry into fraud in the NDIS has made 12 recommendations to improve the integrity of the scheme.

image.altText: Jenny McAllister in the Senate

items.title: Polling whets Labor's appetite for reform

children.content: The slight recovery in Labor's polling shifts assumptions about what's in store for the remainder of this parliamentary term.

items.title: Alcoa to buy South32's WA bauxite mine in $8b deal

children.content: American mining giant Alcoa is set to buy $8 billion worth of assets from Australia's South32. The local shire and workers' union say they were blindsided by the deal.

items.title: Privacy Commissioner ordered to hand over report on Amex security failures

children.content: The Privacy Commissioner has been ordered by parliament to hand over a secret report detailing its investigation into American Express's technology security failures, after using gag orders and legal threats to keep it confidential.

image.altText: Close-up photo of the square blue American Express logo on the corner of a yellow credit card.

children.content: A Labor-led parliamentary inquiry into fraud in the NDIS has made 12 recommendations to improve the integrity of the scheme.

items.title: Quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England claims 'disadvantage'

children.content: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina

children.content: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

articles.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty

children.content: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

items.title: World's biggest porn site puts advertising block on Australian AI empire

children.content: Australian pornography website OurDream has had its advertising suspended by Pornhub after the ABC revealed OurDream's content could include child exploitation material.

items.title: Tough new 'fit and proper person' tests for WA tow truck operators

children.content: The new laws come into effect from today, including "fit and proper person" tests for business operators.

image.altText: A tow truck sits on a flatbed truck.

items.title: When working more hours might mean you lose your home

children.content: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

items.title: Matthew Pavlich reflects on playing in the little-known first Pride game a decade ago

children.content: This Friday will mark 10 years of Sydney Swans Pride games for premiership points.

image.altText: Matthew Pavlich marched in this year's Mardi Gras for the Sydney Swans

items.title: Matthew Flinders’s steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

image.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Jackass began with a gunshot. Now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

image.altText: A bull gores a man dressed as a matador

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

image.altText: Photo illustration with police officer silhouette and text messages from story

items.title: BHP shows off two electric trucks — with 62 diesel ones on order

children.content: BHP and Rio Tinto have put their two battery-electric trucks on show in Western Australia's Pilbara, but is an aversion to risk stifling meaningful change?

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum came from but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Why F1's rumour mill is speculating futures of Piastri and Verstappen

children.content: F1's rumour mill has been in overdrive in recent times about Oscar Piastri's future at McLaren, with his boss having to hose down suggestions the Australian will be driving anything other than a Papaya-coloured F1 car in 2027.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri and Max Verstappen sitting next to each other during the drivers' photo

items.title: Are parents reconsidering letting kids play footy over CTE risks?

children.content: How is the conversation about lasting brain disease from repeated head knocks on the sporting field impacting parents' decisions about what sports their children play?

image.altText: A close shot of paint on the grass at Brookvale Oval.

items.title: 'At greatest risk of CTE': Expert calls out dangers of heading the ball

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

image.altText: A young soccer player about to kick a soccer ball.

items.title: Iconic Gloucester Tree reopens after three-year closure

children.content: Tourism operators are celebrating the reopening of the southern WA landmark, the Gloucester Tree, with visitors making the 37-metre climb to its peak for the first time since its closure three years ago.
